hypothesis: If a Transformer encoder–decoder is pretrained on simulated spectra and fine-tuned with patch-based spectrum embeddings, multitask concentration training, and a jointly trained spectral reconstruction alignment network (with an appropriate reconstruction loss), then it will accurately identify molecular components from IR mixture spectra—including balanced, imbalanced, ternary, and experimental ATR-IR mixtures—achieving high Top-k accuracy and Tanimoto similarity, because the reconstruction loss enforces encoder representations to align with pure-component spectra and thus disentangles overlapping spectral signals.
